{{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan revision.text:
{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan !Brothers and sisters !Owari clan !Brothers and sisters !Imperial line !Brothers and sisters |- | -2 |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] (Nigihayahi [ニギハヤヒ]) | |Ninigi-no-Mikoto [瓊瓊杵命] | |- |1 |Ama-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto [天香語山命] |Younger Brother: Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Ama-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto [天香語山命] |Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Hoori-no-Mikoto [火遠理命] | |- |2 |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] |Younger Brother: Kumanotakakurabe [熊野高倉下] |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] | |Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto [鵜葺草葺不合命] | |- |3 |Yamato-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto [倭宿禰命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto [天忍男命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Jinmu [神武] | |- |4 |Kasamizuhi-no-Mikoto [笠水彦命] | |Ame-no-Tome-no-Mikoto [天戸目命] | |Suizei [綏靖] | |- |5 |Kasatsuhi-no-Mikoto [笠津彦命] | |Take-Tome-no-Mikoto [建斗米命] | |Annei [安寧] | |- |6 |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田勢命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | |Yamato-Tamakushi-no-Mikoto [倭得玉命] | |Kogen [孝安] | |- |9 |Ohonabi-no-Mikoto [意富那比命] |Hime-no-Mikoto [日女命] |Otohiko no Mikoto [弟彦命] | |Korei [孝霊] | |- |10 |Onui-no-Mikoto [乎縫命] | |Awaya-Wake-no-Mikoto [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | |{{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} [建稲種命] | |Sujin [崇神] | |- |13 |Shirituhiko-no-Mikoto [志理都彦命] |Shiritsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尻綱根命] |Otsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尾綱根命] | |Suinin [垂仁] | |- |14 |Kawakami-Maji-no-Mikoto [川上眞稚命] | | | |Seimu [成務] |} ==Notelist== {{notelist}} ==References== {{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{promising draft}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan !Brothers and sisters !Owari clan !Brothers and sisters !Imperial line !Brothers and sisters |- | -2 |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | [天香語山命] |Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Hoori-no-Mikoto [火遠理命] | |- |2 |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] |Younger Brother: Kumanotakakurabe [熊野高倉下] |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] | |Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto [鵜葺草葺不合命] | |- |3 |Yamato-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto [倭宿禰命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto [天忍男命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Jinmu [神武] | |- |4 {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | [天香語山命] |Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Hoori-no-Mikoto [火遠理命] | |- |2 |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] |Younger Brother: Kumanotakakurabe [熊野高倉下] |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] | {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}}{{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan !Brothers and sisters !Owari clan !Brothers and sisters !Imperial line !Brothers and sisters |- | -2 |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] (Nigihayahi [ニギハヤヒ]) | |Ninigi-no-Mikoto [瓊瓊杵命] | |- |1 |Ama-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto [天香語山命] |Younger Brother: Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Ama-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto [天香語山命] |Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Hoori-no-Mikoto [火遠理命] | |- |2 |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] |Younger Brother: Kumanotakakurabe [熊野高倉下] |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] | |Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto [鵜葺草葺不合命] | |- |3 |Yamato-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto [倭宿禰命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto [天忍男命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Jinmu [神武] | |- |4 |Kasamizuhi-no-Mikoto [笠水彦命] | |Ame-no-Tome-no-Mikoto [天戸目命] | |Suizei [綏靖] | |- |5 |Kasatsuhi-no-Mikoto [笠津彦命] | |Take-Tome-no-Mikoto [建斗米命] | |Annei [安寧] | |- |6 |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田勢命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | |Yamato-Tamakushi-no-Mikoto [倭得玉命] | |Kogen [孝安] | |- |9 |Ohonabi-no-Mikoto [意富那比命] |Hime-no-Mikoto [日女命] |Otohiko no Mikoto [弟彦命] | |Korei [孝霊] | |- |10 |Onui-no-Mikoto [乎縫命] | |Awaya-Wake-no-Mikoto [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | |{{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} [建稲種命] | |Sujin [崇神] | |- |13 |Shirituhiko-no-Mikoto [志理都彦命] |Shiritsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尻綱根命] |Otsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尾綱根命] | |Suinin [垂仁] | |- |14 |Kawakami-Maji-no-Mikoto [川上眞稚命] | | | |Seimu [成務] |} ==Notelist== {{notelist}} ==References== {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan !Brothers and sisters !Owari clan !Brothers and sisters !Imperial line !Brothers and sisters |- | -2 |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | [天香語山命] |Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Hoori-no-Mikoto [火遠理命] | |- |2 |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] |Younger Brother: Kumanotakakurabe [熊野高倉下] |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] | |Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto [鵜葺草葺不合命] | |- |3 |Yamato-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto [倭宿禰命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto [天忍男命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Jinmu [神武] | |- |4 |Kasamizuhi-no-Mikoto [笠水彦命] | |Ame-no-Tome-no-Mikoto [天戸目命] | |Suizei [綏靖] | |- |5 |Kasatsuhi-no-Mikoto [笠津彦命] | |Take-Tome-no-Mikoto [建斗米命] | |Annei [安寧] | |- |6 |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田勢命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | |Otsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尾綱根命] | |Suinin [垂仁] | |- |14 |Kawakami-Maji-no-Mikoto [川上眞稚命] | | | |Seimu [成務] |} ==Notelist== {{notelist}} ==References== {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]][[en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan !Brothers and sisters !Owari clan !Brothers and sisters !Imperial line !Brothers and sisters |- | -2 |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] (Nigihayahi [ニギハヤヒ]) | |Ninigi-no-Mikoto [瓊瓊杵命] | |- |1 |Ama-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto [天香語山命] |Younger Brother: Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Ama-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto [天香語山命] |Kamimimatanomikoto [可美眞手命] |Hoori-no-Mikoto [火遠理命] | |- |2 |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] |Younger Brother: Kumanotakakurabe [熊野高倉下] |Ama-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto [天村雲命] | |Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto [鵜葺草葺不合命] | |- |3 |Yamato-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto [倭宿禰命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto [天忍男命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Jinmu [神武] | |- |4 |Kasamizuhi-no-Mikoto [笠水彦命] | |Ame-no-Tome-no-Mikoto [天戸目命] | |Suizei [綏靖] | |- |5 |Kasatsuhi-no-Mikoto [笠津彦命] | |Take-Tome-no-Mikoto [建斗米命] | |Annei [安寧] | |- |6 |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田勢命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | |Yamato-Tamakushi-no-Mikoto [倭得玉命] | |Kogen [孝安] | |- |9 |Ohonabi-no-Mikoto [意富那比命] |Hime-no-Mikoto [日女命] |Otohiko no Mikoto [弟彦命] | |Korei [孝霊] | |- |10 |Onui-no-Mikoto [乎縫命] | |Awaya-Wake-no-Mikoto [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | |{{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} [建稲種命] | |Sujin [崇神] | |- |13 |Shirituhiko-no-Mikoto [志理都彦命] |Shiritsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尻綱根命] |Otsuna-ne-no-Mikoto [尾綱根命] | |Suinin [垂仁] | |- |14 |Kawakami-Maji-no-Mikoto [川上眞稚命] | | | |Seimu [成務] |} ==Notelist== {{notelist}} ==References== {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters.
