{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}} | cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book |last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book |last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org}} |- !Number !Amabe clan |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|12=draft}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|12=draft}} |- !Number {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|12=draft}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|12=draft}} |- !Number {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|12=draft}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}} | title = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|12=draft}} | noble family = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|12=draft}} |- !Number | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} | title = {{ill||Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|12=draft|12=draft}} | noble family = {{ill||Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill||Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|12=draft|12=draft}} |- {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeinadane]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=draft|12=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|12=draft|12=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=draft|12=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|12=draft|12=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=draft|12=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=draft|12=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|12=draft|12=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|12=draft|12=draft}} |- [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Takeinadane|Takeinadane]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | revision.comment: 30 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:建稲種命 up to rev 97912424
revision.text:
'''建稲種命'''（たけいなだねのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[尾張国造]]'''の一人。『[[古事記]]』では'''建伊那陀宿禰'''（たけいなだのすくね）とも称する。
== 概要 == 父は[[尾張国造]]・[[乎止与命]]、母は[[眞敷刀婢命]]（[[尾張大印岐]]の女）で、[[宮簀媛]]は[[妹]]。[[妃]]の[[玉姫]]（丹羽氏の祖[[大荒田命]]の女）との間に二男四女がある。息子[[尻綱根命]]は[[応神天皇]]の大臣。その下の娘[[志理都紀斗売]]は[[五百城入彦皇子]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子）の妃で、[[品陀真若王]]の母。更にその下の娘[[金田屋野姫命]]は品陀真若王の妃で、応神天皇の[[皇后]][[仲姫命]]及び2人の妃の母。
景行天皇と[[成務天皇]]の二代の間、[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]に仕え、[[ヤマトタケル]]東征の際、副将軍として軍を従え、軍功を挙げたとされる。[[熱田神宮]]・[[内々神社]]・[[幡頭神社]]・[[羽豆神社]]・[[成海神社]]・[[尾張戸神社]]・[[八雲神社 (八雲町)|八雲神社]]などに祭られている。
== 系譜 == *父：[[乎止与命]]（[[天火明命]]の子孫） *母：[[眞敷刀婢命]]（[[大印岐]]の女） *妹：[[宮簀媛]]（[[日本武尊]]の妃） *妃：[[玉姫]]（[[大荒田命]]の女） **[[尾綱根命]] **[[志理都紀斗売]]（[[五百城入彦皇子]]の妃） ***[[品陀真若王]] **[[金田屋野姫命]]（品陀真若王の妃） ***[[高城入姫命]]（[[応神天皇]]の妃） ***[[仲姫命]]（応神天皇の皇后、[[仁徳天皇]]の母） ***[[弟姫命]]（応神天皇の妃）
== 関連項目 == *[[内々神社]] - 東征の帰路、水軍を率いていた建稲種命がめずらしい海鳥を見つけて、ヤマトタケルに献上しようと、捕まえようとして[[駿河国|駿河]]の海に落ち水死し、ヤマトタケルが東征の帰路で尾張の[[内津峠]]に入った後、早馬で駆けてきた従者の[[久米八腹]]から、建稲種命が水死した報告を聞き、悲泣して「うつつかな、うつつかな」といわれその霊を祀ったのが起源と伝えられている。 *[[幡頭神社]] - 三河湾（渥美湾）の[[西尾市]]（吉良町）にある宮崎という地にある岬の丘の地に鎮座。東征の帰路、「幡頭」を務めた建稲種命が駿河（伊豆）沖の海で水難事故で死に、遺骸がこの岬の宮崎海岸に流れ着き、村人達により葬られたと伝えられる。 *待合浦（[[羽豆岬]]） - 南知多町師崎にある建稲種命を祀る[[羽豆神社]]近く、師崎は建稲種命と妻の玉姫が住んでいたとされ、建稲種命は毎日の様に風光明媚な羽豆岬を散歩していたとされる。やがて建稲種命がヤマトタケルに従い水軍を率いて東征の出陣の時、妻の玉姫は夫の帰りをずっと待ち続けていたとされる浦の為、「待合浦」と呼ばれる様になった。
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[平凡社]]編『[[神道大辞典]] 第二卷』平凡社、[[1941年]]、430頁（[{{NDLDC|1913348/250}} 「神道大辞典 : 3巻. 第二卷」近代デジタルライブラリー]）。

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[建稲種命]] to [[Takeinadane]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''建稲種命'''（たけいなだねのみこと）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[尾張国造]]'''の一人。『[[古事記]]』では'''建伊那陀宿禰'''（たけいなだのすくね）とも称する。
== 概要 == 父は[[尾張国造]]・[[乎止与命]]、母は[[眞敷刀婢命]]（[[尾張大印岐]]の女）で、[[宮簀媛]]は[[妹]]。[[妃]]の[[玉姫]]（丹羽氏の祖[[大荒田命]]の女）との間に二男四女がある。息子[[尻綱根命]]は[[応神天皇]]の大臣。その下の娘[[志理都紀斗売]]は[[五百城入彦皇子]]（[[景行天皇]]皇子）の妃で、[[品陀真若王]]の母。更にその下の娘[[金田屋野姫命]]は品陀真若王の妃で、応神天皇の[[皇后]][[仲姫命]]及び2人の妃の母。
景行天皇と[[成務天皇]]の二代の間、[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]に仕え、[[ヤマトタケル]]東征の際、副将軍として軍を従え、軍功を挙げたとされる。[[熱田神宮]]・[[内々神社]]・[[幡頭神社]]・[[羽豆神社]]・[[成海神社]]・[[尾張戸神社]]・[[八雲神社 (八雲町)|八雲神社]]などに祭られている。
== 系譜 == *父：[[乎止与命]]（[[天火明命]]の子孫） *母：[[眞敷刀婢命]]（[[大印岐]]の女） *妹：[[宮簀媛]]（[[日本武尊]]の妃） *妃：[[玉姫]]（[[大荒田命]]の女） **[[尾綱根命]] **[[志理都紀斗売]]（[[五百城入彦皇子]]の妃） ***[[品陀真若王]] **[[金田屋野姫命]]（品陀真若王の妃） ***[[高城入姫命]]（[[応神天皇]]の妃） ***[[仲姫命]]（応神天皇の皇后、[[仁徳天皇]]の母） ***[[弟姫命]]（応神天皇の妃）
== 関連項目 == *[[内々神社]] - 東征の帰路、水軍を率いていた建稲種命がめずらしい海鳥を見つけて、ヤマトタケルに献上しようと、捕まえようとして[[駿河国|駿河]]の海に落ち水死し、ヤマトタケルが東征の帰路で尾張の[[内津峠]]に入った後、早馬で駆けてきた従者の[[久米八腹]]から、建稲種命が水死した報告を聞き、悲泣して「うつつかな、うつつかな」といわれその霊を祀ったのが起源と伝えられている。 *[[幡頭神社]] - 三河湾（渥美湾）の[[西尾市]]（吉良町）にある宮崎という地にある岬の丘の地に鎮座。東征の帰路、「幡頭」を務めた建稲種命が駿河（伊豆）沖の海で水難事故で死に、遺骸がこの岬の宮崎海岸に流れ着き、村人達により葬られたと伝えられる。 *待合浦（[[羽豆岬]]） - 南知多町師崎にある建稲種命を祀る[[羽豆神社]]近く、師崎は建稲種命と妻の玉姫が住んでいたとされ、建稲種命は毎日の様に風光明媚な羽豆岬を散歩していたとされる。やがて建稲種命がヤマトタケルに従い水軍を率いて東征の出陣の時、妻の玉姫は夫の帰りをずっと待ち続けていたとされる浦の為、「待合浦」と呼ばれる様になった。
== 脚注 == <references/>
== 参考文献 == * [[平凡社]]編『[[神道大辞典]] 第二卷』平凡社、[[1941年]]、430頁（[{{NDLDC|1913348/250}} 「神道大辞典 : 3巻. 第二卷」近代デジタルライブラリー]）。

{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{complex}} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|complete=true|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|ja|宮簀媛|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Miyazu-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki.
