{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|qid=Q110930985}}; {{ill|Q110931035|qid=Q110931035}}; {{ill|Q110931157|qid=Q110931157}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|qid=Q97706258}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11465294}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11486691]] Wikidata name: Takeinadane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- {{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|qid=Q110930985}}; {{ill|Q110931035|qid=Q110931035}}; {{ill|Q110931157|qid=Q110931157}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|qid=Q97706258}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11465294}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11486691]] Wikidata name: Takeinadane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|qid=Q110930985}}; {{ill|Q110931035|qid=Q110931035}}; {{ill|Q110931157|qid=Q110931157}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|qid=Q97706258}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|qid=Q11455216}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11465294}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11486691]] Wikidata name: Takeinadane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. {{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |- [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | |Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto [鵜葺草葺不合命] | |- |3 |Yamato-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto [倭宿禰命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Ame-no-Oshio-no-Mikoto [天忍男命] |Ame-no-Oshihito-no-Mikoto [天忍人命] |Jinmu [神武] | |- |4 |Kasamizuhi-no-Mikoto [笠水彦命] | |Ame-no-Tome-no-Mikoto [天戸目命] | |Suizei [綏靖] | |- |5 |Kasatsuhi-no-Mikoto [笠津彦命] | |Take-Tome-no-Mikoto [建斗米命] | |Annei [安寧] | |- |6 |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田勢命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | |Yamato-Tamakushi-no-Mikoto [倭得玉命] | |Kogen [孝安] | |- |9 |Ohonabi-no-Mikoto [意富那比命] |Hime-no-Mikoto [日女命] |Otohiko no Mikoto [弟彦命] | |Korei [孝霊] | |- |10 |Onui-no-Mikoto [乎縫命] | |Awaya-Wake-no-Mikoto [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | |{{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} [建稲種命] | |Sujin [崇神] | |- |13 It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|WD=Q110930985}}; {{ill|Q110931035|WD=Q110931035}}; {{ill|Q110931157|WD=Q110931157}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|WD=Q97706258}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11486691]] Wikidata name: Takeinadane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the [[:en:2nd century|second century]]. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and [[:en:Miyazu-hime|Miyazu-hime]] and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|WD=Q110930985}}; {{ill|Q110931035|WD=Q110931035}}; {{ill|Q110931157|WD=Q110931157}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|WD=Q97706258}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11486691]] Wikidata name: Takeinadane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:29, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takeinadane]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number !Amabe clan !Brothers and sisters !Owari clan !Brothers and sisters !Imperial line !Brothers and sisters |- | -2 |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |Amaterasu Omikami [天照大神] | |- | -1 |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto [天忍穂耳命] | |- | |↓ | | | | | |- |Ancestor |Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto [天火明天命] | |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | |Yamato-Tamakushi-no-Mikoto [倭得玉命] | |Kogen [孝安] | |- |9 |Ohonabi-no-Mikoto [意富那比命] |Hime-no-Mikoto [日女命] |Otohiko no Mikoto [弟彦命] | |Korei [孝霊] | |- |10 |Onui-no-Mikoto [乎縫命] | |Awaya-Wake-no-Mikoto
