{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|WD=Q110930985}}; {{ill|Q110931035|WD=Q110931035}}; {{ill|Q110931157|WD=Q110931157}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|WD=Q97706258}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294}}}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|WD=Q110930985|1=Shiratsunane no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q110931035|WD=Q110931035|1=Shiritsukitome}}; {{ill|Q110931157|WD=Q110931157|1=Owari no Tadashi no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|WD=Q97706258|1=Otoyo no mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|1=Miyazu-hime}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century|1=2nd century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number |Take-Tase-no-Mikoto [建田背命] | |Itoku [懿徳] | |- |7 |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] |Toyotama-hime [豊玉姫] |Take-Morosumi-no-Mikoto [建諸隅命] | |Kosho [孝昭] | |- |8 |Nihon-Tamamitsu-no-Mikoto [日本得魂命] | [淡夜別命] | |Kogen [孝元] | |- |11 |Otoyo-no-Mikoto [小登与命] | |Kotoyo-no-Mikoto [乎止輿命] | |Kaika [開化] | |- |12 |Take-Inadane-no-Mikoto [建稲種命] | [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|WD=Q110930985|1=Shiratsunane no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q110931035|WD=Q110931035|1=Shiritsukitome}}; {{ill|Q110931157|WD=Q110931157|1=Owari no Tadashi no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|WD=Q97706258|1=Otoyo no mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|1=Miyazu-hime}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century|1=2nd century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] [[fr:Takeinadane]][[ja:建稲種命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q110930985|WD=Q110930985|1=Shiratsunane no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q110931035|WD=Q110931035|1=Shiritsukitome}}; {{ill|Q110931157|WD=Q110931157|1=Owari no Tadashi no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|WD=Q97706258|1=Otoyo no mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|1=Miyazu-hime}}|organization={{ill|Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}}} {{infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = {{ill|Otoyo no mikoto|ja|乎止与命|complete=true|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoyo no mikoto|qq=}} | cult_centre = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}},
{{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century|1=2nd century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number {{ill|Hakkengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakkengū|qq=}}|cult_center = {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} | title = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | CoA = | tenure = | predecessor = | successor = {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} | noble family = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari clan|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | spouse = Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto) | god_of = {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|fr|clan Owari|simple|User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} }} {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} was a Japanese warrior in the {{ill|2nd century|en|2nd century|WD=Q8103|lt=second century|1=2nd century}}. He is worshipped as a {{ill|kami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite web |title=Introduction {{!}} ATSUTA JINGU |url=https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/en/intro/ |access-date=2023-12-09 |website=www.atsutajingu.or.jp}} He was the brother of {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} the founder of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}.{{Cite book|last=Ponsonby-Fane |first=R. A. B. |title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines |date=2016-05-11 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-138-98322-9 |edition=1st |language=English |chapter=Atsuta Jingu|qq=draft}}{{rp|435}} {{Dead link|date=December 2023}} The {{ill|Owari clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Owari|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}} established the {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} in 192, and held the position of the shrine's high priest since ancient times, passing it down from generation to generation.{{Cite book|last=Naito |first=Toho |title=Choshu Zasshi (張州雑志) |publisher=Aichi-ken Kyōdo Shiryō Kankō-kai |year=1975 |doi=10.11501/9537297|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} and {{ill|Miyazu-hime|en|Miyazu-hime|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime|1=Miyazu-hime}} and her brother {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|en:draft|Takeinadane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} are worshipped at the shrine.{{rp|429}} He is also worshipped at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} alongside his wife Tamahime.{{Cite web|title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US|qq=draft}} He and his wife had two sons and four daughters. After his death his wife set up {{ill|Tagata Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in his honor among other deities. ==Genealogy== {|class="wikitable" ! colspan="7" |Source{{Cite web|date=2024-01-12|title=海部氏系図Ａ|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112203311/http://houki.yonago-kodaisi.com/F-BG-amabe.html|access-date=2024-05-20|website=web.archive.org|qq=draft}} |- !Number
